mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and strong consumer demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the medium term. This reflects a healthy economic expansion.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at a very low level, it signals an accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns as discount rates for future earnings decline.
regime_hint: monetary easing or very low rate environment
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0% for a sustained period (e.g., 12 months) but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently negative or flat.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust industrial production (INDPRO) indicates broad economic strength, suggesting healthy consumer and business activity. This translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the short-to-medium term.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and INDPRO show strong growth for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 70% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) during an economic downturn signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced spending. This negatively impacts corporate revenues and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises significantly (e.g., >0.5pp over 3 months) during a confirmed recession, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently positive or flat.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, provides the Federal Reserve with flexibility to maintain accommodative policies or ease further. This supports asset valuations by keeping interest rates low and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual growth remains below 2% for 12 consecutive months, but S&P 500 5-day returns are predominantly negative in those periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a weakening housing market and broader economic slowdown. This can spill over into other sectors, reducing overall demand and negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 65% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a tightening federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) suggests the Fed is actively combating an overheating economy. This typically leads to higher discount rates and a contraction in equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: If both inflation is high and the Fed is tightening, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently positive in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic growth, as indicated by strong GDP expansion (GDPC1), corporate earnings tend to be higher. This supports equity prices and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence increases.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 annual growth exceeds 3% for two consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of those periods.
